title: Phrynium longibracteatum
taxonomy: Phrynium longibracteatum is a species of flowering plant in the family Marantaceae. This plant is native to Southeast Asia and is a part of the large and diverse genus Phrynium. The genus Phrynium is further classified under the order Zingiberales, which includes other plants like ginger and heliconia. The family Marantaceae is a group of flowering plants commonly known as prayer-plants or philodendrons, due to their unique leaf structure. Phrynium longibracteatum is a relatively rare and understudied species, with limited information available on its classification and characteristics.
